content: When a change spans two repos but the working environment can only push to one (e.g. a Codespace token scoped to a single repo returns 403 on the sibling repo), don't silently drop the cross-repo half. Record the exact change as a durable, self-contained handoff in a place the other repo's maintainer will see: the full patch (git-am-able), the precise insertion anchors (file + the lines it sits between), the base commit, and a copy-pasteable manual fallback. A PR comment on the merged work is a good carrier. The receiving repo then applies it as a normal ticket, cross-linking the source issue.
context: T-032 (2026-06-22): presales-sqnce #115 had a cross-repo half — a generation-time input-contract audit step for je-dev-skills' create-dataset skill. It was committed in the presales Codespace (branch 115-input-contract-audit, e4f6270) but the token was scoped to presales-sqnce, so push to dawtips/je-dev-skills 403'd. The author left a PR-116 comment with both an apply-the-patch path and a paste-manually path, exact anchors, and base commit d55339f. Applying it here was mechanical and verbatim; lint + all offline suites stayed green.
title: Cross-repo change you can't push: leave a self-contained, apply-able handoff
